name: State Of Art Transport New
recent_news: No public news, M&A, or district contracts found; appears to be a small private operator in Plantation/Broward County area.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~15 employees, ~$1M rev) Broward County operator; potential micro tuck-in if it has ESE/special-needs district routes, but no confirmed school-bus contract footprint and very low confidence in even being an active operator vs. broker.

name: Tropical Transportation LLC
recent_news: No public M&A, news, or contract awards found; not to be confused with Tropical Shipping (West Palm Beach freight carrier).
fit_notes: Small Palm Beach County private LLC, likely paratransit/NEMT subcontractor under Palm Tran Connection or Medicaid broker; sub-scale for Summit unless tied to PBC school district SPED contract — not confirmed.

name: A-1 Transporters
districts_or_customers: Likely Verida/LogistiCare Georgia Medicaid NEMT broker subcontractor in SE Georgia (Ware County region)
recent_news: No public news; confirmed as medical/NEMT transport provider in Waycross, GA per Yelp and business directories.
fit_notes: Confirmed owner-operator (Gary Hunter) suggests classic succession candidate, but A-1 Transporters appears to be NEMT/medical transport rather than school-bus contractor. Likely off-strategy for Summit unless it also runs SPED routes for Ware County Schools — not evidenced.

name: Promise Transportation
recent_news: No public news, contract awards, or M&A found. Multiple unrelated 'Promise' carriers exist in FMCSA data but none clearly match this Jacksonville address.
fit_notes: Sub-scale Jacksonville private operator at office-suite address (Suite 106); profile fits a small NEMT/paratransit subcontractor, not a Duval County Schools bus contractor. Low priority for Summit tuck-in.

name: Sol Transportation Inc
districts_or_customers: San Diego County area school districts (per company site)
recent_news: Company history page indicates steady organic expansion since 2007 (LA founding) into San Diego (2008), VA federal contract (2011), and gurney transport (2016). No M&A activity disclosed.
fit_notes: Independent, ~19-year-old SoCal special-needs/NEMT operator with VA + school district mix; California is outside Summit's core in_summit_state footprint per source data (expansion_state=0). Tuck-in fit limited by geography and SPED/NEMT-skew vs. yellow-bus core; revisit only if Summit pursues CA SPED expansion.
